programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: MYSTERIES OF HIDDEN ROOMS
programme.desc: Throughout history, humanity has been captivated by hidden places—rooms, passages, and chambers. Whether for escape or sinister motives, these ten notable hidden spaces span the ages, revealing intrigue and mystery.

programme.title: Myth Hunters
programme.desc: The Honjo Masamune, Japan's greatest sword, vanished after WWII. Taken as a souvenir by a US Sergeant, it's now a legendary lost artifact sought by collectors worldwide.

programme.title: Myth Hunters
programme.desc: A vast treasure of Spanish gold is buried somewhere in Texas. Dave Arnold, guided by a map, discovers three mysterious stones he believes are keys to the treasure. His 7-year quest leads to a mysterious death, financial ruin, and an ancient curse.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: SECRET PROJECTS AND OPERATIONS
programme.desc: Imagine a world with open use of resources from secret government projects. Little is known about these black ops. They hold mysteries and consequences, challenging democratic ideals. History reveals human experimentation, contrary to official claims.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: SUPRESSED INVENTIONS IN HISTORY
programme.desc: Explore lost or suppressed inventions from history, some skeptical, others mysterious. Despite doubts, many are useful today. Stay open-minded as we delve into these intriguing relics.

programme.title: Myth Hunters
programme.desc: German adventurer Heinrich Schlieman embarks on a lifelong quest to find the mythical city of Troy and its fabulous treasures.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the Metropolitan Museum, New York
programme.desc: From the mystery of a king’s madness to a silver chalice some think is the Holy Grail, discover the secrets inside the Metropolitan Museum.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the Imperial War Museum, London
programme.desc: From Churchill risking his life in the Blitz to 21st Century soldiers who become invisible, discover the secrets inside the Imperial War Museum.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the National Museum of Anthropology, Mexico City
programme.desc: From ancient athletes who kick a severed head to a peasant dress that brings down an empire, discover the secrets inside the National Museum of Anthropology.

programme.title: Murder Maps: In The Shadow Of Jack
programme.desc: At the time of Jack The Ripper, London was home to some of the most terrible individuals the city has ever seen. One of the very worst was the elusive Borough Poisoner, George Chapman.

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: It was a crime which shocked Britain. In 1878, a widow was killed in her own home, the killer was her own servant. For the young servant did not only kill and dismember her employer – she stole her clothes, her property and her identity.

programme.title: Murder Maps: The Lady Killer
programme.desc: As Europe recovered from the damage of WWII, soldiers made their way home from the front line. But one man, brought back all the savagery and brutality of the fighting.

programme.title: scienceNOW: How Does the Brain Work?
programme.desc: Hosted by Neil deGrasse Tyson, examine these topics: Magic and the Brains; Can a Machine Think Like Us?; Magnetic Mind Control; and a profile of neuroscientist David Eagleman, who studies time perception and synesthesia.

programme.title: Naked Science: Snowball Earth
programme.desc: There is a theory that scientists once dismissed as absurd - that long ago the Earth became so cold every inch of it was entombed in ice.

programme.title: Naked Science: How To Avert an Asteroid Impact
programme.desc: Throughout history, the earth has been bombarded from space. Asteroid impacts have caused mass extinctions – including the dinosaur wipe out.

programme.title: scienceNOW: What Will the Future Be Like?
programme.desc: The technologies that will transform our lives decades from now are already taking shape in laboratories around the world. Segments include: Humanoid Robots; Mind-Reading Machines; Augmented Reality; Profile - Adrien Treuille.

programme.title: Naked Science: Anatomy Of Tornado
programme.desc: Tornados are one of the most destructive forces of the planets with winds at 300 miles an hour. They can destroy buildings and hurl cars hundreds of feet through the air.

programme.title: scienceNOW: Can We Live Forever?
programme.desc: NOVA scienceNOW tackles the big question: can we live forever? Hosted by astrophysicist Neil deGrasse Tyson, examine these topics: Can My Car Live Forever?; Body Shop - Body Parts; Can We Slow Aging?; and Hibernation.

programme.title: Britain's Bloody Crown
programme.desc: Did Richard III really kill the 'Princes in the Tower'?

programme.title: Britain's Bloody Crown
programme.desc: Dan Jones reveals the remarkable story of Henry VII's mother.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: Tony travels to Mexico City and Oaxaca to meet who fight for the food, the art, and the lives they believe in. In the Santa Anita neighbourhood of Mexico City, Tony meets boxer Jorge Lacierva.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: Tony visits Andalucia during Semana Santa – Holy Week – a time filled with great pageantry and excitement. Tony’s longtime DP, Zach Zamboni, currently lives in Granada with his fiancée, Fuen Sanchez.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: For the first time, Tony visits Budapest, 25 years after democracy planted its flag –alongside the Oscar-winning master of light, cinematographer Vilmos Zsigmond, as one of his key guides.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
